reforms.description: The Irish government has linked industrial support grants more closely to employment creation, aiming to improve the cost-effectiveness of the industrial support programme.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By linking grants to employment creation, the reform is expected to enhance productivity and efficiency in the industrial sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been put into practice as part of the government's industrial support strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant adjustment in the approach to industrial support, focusing on employment outcomes.
reforms.source_quote: Greater cost-effectiveness has been imposed on the industrial support programme by linking grants more closely to employment creation.

reforms.description: The introduction of a new Competition Act in 1991 extended EC competition law principles to the domestic economy, making anti-competitive practices illegal.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enforcing competition laws, the reform is expected to foster a more competitive market environment, which can lead to increased innovation and efficiency.
reforms.status_evidence: The Competition Act has been enacted and is currently in force, regulating anti-competitive practices.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a major overhaul of competition law, significantly impacting the regulatory landscape.
reforms.source_quote: The Act came into force 1 October 1991, when most of the provisions of the Restrictive Practices Acts were repealed.
